breaking.text: CEO Hock Tan projects $100B+ AI chip revenue pipeline citing Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI as key customers
Broadcom posts record Q1 revenue of $19.3B, AI revenue soars 106% to $8.4B; stock up 5% after hours
Fed Beige Book flags weaker consumer spending and sticky energy/labor costs across multiple districts
Broadcom surges 5% after hours on blowout Q1: AI revenue up 106% to $8.4B, CEO touts $100B AI chip revenue pipeline
Broadcom reports fiscal Q1 earnings after the bell — analysts expect $2.03 EPS on $19.18B revenue

summary: U.S. equities staged a broad recovery on Wednesday as easing oil prices and resilient economic data calmed investor nerves following the Iran-related selloff. The S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,869.50, the Nasdaq gained 1.29%, and the Dow added 238 points, led by a tech rebound with Micron and AMD each up over 5%. After the close, Broadcom delivered a blockbuster Q1 report with record $19.3B revenue and AI revenue surging 106% to $8.4B, guiding Q2 to $22B. Earlier in the session, Ross Stores surged 7.4% on blowout Q4 earnings, Coinbase jumped 12% after Trump backed crypto firms over banks on stablecoin legislation, and BofA initiated Tesla at Buy with a $460 target, while Dubai stocks crashed 4.7% as UAE markets reopened after a two-day Iran-conflict closure.

highlights.text: Dubai stocks crash 4.7% — worst since 2022 — as UAE markets reopen after two-day closure following Iranian drone and missile strikes
Coinbase jumps 12% after Trump publicly backs crypto firms over banks on stablecoin yield, urging passage of digital asset market structure bill
BofA initiates Tesla at Buy with $460 price target, calling it the clear leader in autonomous driving — TSLA gains 3.6% on the session
Ross Stores surges 7.4% after Q4 EPS of $2.00 beats estimates; comp sales up 9% — plans 110 new stores in 2026 and 10% dividend hike
Broadcom Q1 revenue hits record $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue surges 106% to $8.4B; guides Q2 to $22B with AI revenue expected at $10.7B — stock up 5% after hours
Bank of America initiates Tesla at Buy with $460 price target, citing robotaxi and autonomous driving upside
CoreWeave jumps 8% after securing multi-year deal to power Perplexity's AI inference on Nvidia Grace Blackwell clusters
GitLab plunges 13.5% to 52-week low of $23.41 after weak FY2027 guidance and fears that AI coding agents threaten its core business
Fed Beige Book shows weaker consumer weighing on sales across multiple districts, with sticky price pressures from energy and labor costs
Broadcom guides Q2 revenue to $22B, crushing $20.56B consensus; announces $10B share buyback program; stock up 5% after hours
Broadcom Q1 revenue hits record $19.3B (up 29% YoY), AI revenue soars 106% to $8.4B; CEO Hock Tan projects $100B AI chip revenue pipeline from Anthropic, Meta, OpenAI
Consumer discretionary posts best session since Oct 31 led by Ross Stores +7%
CoreWeave jumps 8% after announcing multiyear cloud agreement with Perplexity
ISM Services PMI at 53.5 with cooling prices-paid component suggests service-sector inflation may be peaking
Fed Beige Book shows 7 of 12 districts with slight-to-moderate growth, but 5 districts flat or declining
Crypto stocks surge — Coinbase +14.6%, MicroStrategy +10.4%, Robinhood +8.1% — on Trump digital asset bill support
ADP private payrolls beat expectations at 63K vs 50K forecast, easing growth-scare fears
CoreWeave jumps 8% on multiyear agreement with Perplexity; BofA initiates Tesla at Buy with $460 target
Consumer discretionary sector pops 2% for best day since Oct 31 — Ross Stores +7%, Amazon/Tesla/Airbnb all +3%
GitLab tumbles 8% premarket on disappointing FY revenue guidance of $1.10-1.12B vs $1.12B expected
